transit.title:
Shinkansen to
the snow.
transit.lead: About three hours from Tokyo. Exit Sichinohe-Towada Station and a roughly 10-minute taxi ride—transit-resort travel with minimal friction.
transit.imgAlt: Travel image of snowy scenery seen from a Tohoku Shinkansen car

primary.title: About 10 minutes from the Shinkansen
primary.lead: Roughly 10 minutes by taxi from Tohoku Shinkansen Sichinohe-Towada Station. A municipal ski area that's easy to stop at mid-journey. At ¥2,500 for a day ticket, even a short visit pays for itself.
primary.imgAlt: Travel image of snowy scenery seen from a Shinkansen car

secondary.title: To a gensen onsen paradise
secondary.lead: After skiing, soak at Higashi-Hakkoda Onsen where 48°C spring water flows straight into the bath. From around ¥300 for adults—enjoy silky water at a local sento alongside residents.

onsen.title:
An area where
gensen flows daily
onsen.lead: Around Sichinohe, onsen lovers call it a "gensen paradise." Moor springs and simple alkaline springs—some facilities pour amber water generously from tub to shower.
onsen.whisper: From around ¥300 entry, hopping between two or three local baths after skiing is a classic post-run ritual.
onsen.imgAlt: Image of an outdoor bath surrounded by forest

meta.title: 3 nearby onsen — Sichinohe Town Ski Area
meta.description: Higashi-Hakkoda, Tsuta, and Towadako — gensen onsen guide with area map

hero.title:
After skiing,
soak in gensen.
hero.tagline: 10 min from Shinkansen — onsen loop around Sichinohe and Towada
hero.imgAlt: Image of an onsen at the Hakkoda foothills

nearbyOnsen.notice: Day-use hours, winter closures, and road conditions change—check each facility's official site. Higashi-Hakkoda Onsen may reorganize operations; confirm before you visit.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: Around Sichinohe, local sento with gensen flow sit close to forest hideaway baths.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Three baths within roughly 30–50 minutes by car from the ski area, ordered for a post-run loop.
nearbyOnsen.mapLead: Toggle food, onsen, and anchor layers—or show several at once.
nearbyOnsen.mapEmbedTitle: Sichinohe area food, onsen, and hubs map

mapLayers.hint: Food and onsen layers can be shown together
mapLayers.mobileHint: If pins overlap, use “View on map” below or the spot list on the map

nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: From a station-side sento to a secret forest bath and lakeside spas—easy to slot after skiing.

higashi-hakkoda.title: Higashi-Hakkoda Onsen
higashi-hakkoda.lead: Alkaline simple spring at about 47°C, poured straight into the tubs. Hiba bath, reclining tub, and cold plunge. About 5 minutes on foot from Sichinohe-Towada Station. Day use around ¥400 for adults, 9:00–21:00 (per official info).
higashi-hakkoda.imgAlt: Hiba bath and indoor tubs at Higashi-Hakkoda Onsen

tsuta.title: Tsuta Onsen Ryokan
tsuta.lead: Rare baths where water wells up through the tub floor. A single-inn retreat in beech forest. Day use ¥1,000, 10:00–15:00 (last entry 14:30). Long winter closure—check the official site before visiting.
tsuta.imgAlt: Wooden bath hall at Tsuta Onsen Ryokan in the forest

yakeyama.title: Towadako Onsen
yakeyama.lead: Spa cluster around Yasumiya on Lake Towada. Hotels such as Hotel Towadaso offer day bathing—handy before or after lake and Oirase outings.
yakeyama.imgAlt: Snow-view outdoor bath overlooking Lake Towada

nearbyOnsen.pairingLead: Morning: Shinkansen from Tokyo → afternoon: Sichinohe slopes → evening: Higashi-Hakkoda → next day: Tsuta or Lake Towada.

meta.title: 2 nearby food spots — Sichinohe Town Ski Area
meta.description: Slope-side hutte and Michi no Eki Shichinohe — food guide with area map

hero.title:
After snow,
eat local.
hero.tagline: Slope classics to horse-meat ramen—all near the station
hero.imgAlt: Image of a skier on the slopes

nearbyFood.notice: Hours, holidays, and menus change—check each venue's official information.
nearbyFood.introLead: Sichinohe pairs a simple slope cafeteria with Michi no Eki dining a short walk from the Shinkansen stop.
nearbyFood.introBody: Lunch on the hill and a reward meal on the way back—two stops matched to a ski day.
nearbyFood.mapLead: Toggle food, onsen, and anchor layers—or show several at once.
nearbyFood.mapEmbedTitle: Sichinohe area food, onsen, and hubs map

mapLayers.hint: Food and onsen layers can be shown together
mapLayers.mobileHint: If pins overlap, use “View on map” below or the spot list on the map

nearbyFood.spotsLead: From on-slope lunch to station-side Michi no Eki—ordered for a snow day.

hutte.title: Town Hutte
hutte.lead: Cafeteria beside the first pair lift. Soy ramen ¥700, curry rice ¥700, beef bowl ¥600, and other slope staples. Weekdays 10:30–14:00, weekends & holidays 10:30–15:00 (per official info).
hutte.imgAlt: Soy ramen at the town hutte slope cafeteria

tabikura.title: Tabikura (Michi no Eki Shichinohe)
tabikura.lead: Restaurant Ema inside the Tabikura hall is known for horse-meat ramen, plus local keiran and hand-cut soba. About 5 minutes on foot from Sichinohe-Towada Station; restaurant weekdays 11:00–15:00 (per official info).
tabikura.imgAlt: Horse-meat ramen at Michi no Eki Shichinohe restaurant Ema

nearbyFood.pairingLead: Morning: Shinkansen to Sichinohe-Towada → lunch: hutte ramen → afternoon: ski → return: horse ramen at Michi no Eki → evening: Higashi-Hakkoda Onsen.

powder.title: Powder from the station
powder.body: Champion Course up to 35° on ungroomed snow. Fresh deep snow without powder panic. Also serves as a backup slope when Hakkoda is closed.

shinkansen.title: Go by Shinkansen
shinkansen.body: Tokyo ~3 hours → station → 10-minute taxi. A strategic stop on a Honshu–Hokkaido route with JR EAST PASS. Design a day of extremes: Tokyo morning, slopes afternoon, onsen evening.

beginners.title: Beginners & families
beginners.body: A wide groomed run with a maximum pitch of 10°. Free night-lesson Thursdays from 19:00 and simple lodge food—an authentic local guide for newcomers.

access.note: About 10 minutes by taxi from Tohoku Shinkansen Sichinohe-Towada Station (approx.)
